response:
Sec. 1  
People who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their bodies react to the protein in peanuts when they come into contact with it  
This reaction can occur even if the peanuts are not ingested directly  
Underwater environments may contain traces of peanuts or peanut products  
These traces can trigger an allergic reaction even in small amounts  
Living underwater does not eliminate the risk of exposure to peanuts  

Sec. 2  
Allergic reactions to peanuts are immune system responses to specific proteins found in the nuts  
These proteins are not affected by water or environmental conditions  
The body's immune system does not differentiate between the presence of peanuts in water or other forms  
Therefore people with peanut allergies must avoid all forms of peanut exposure regardless of their environment  
Living underwater does not provide a safe haven from peanut allergens
